sentence_reviews.text: Reliability and cost operations manage failures, replication, quotas, capacity, security, energy, carbon, and economic efficiency across AI workloads.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports failures, replication, capacity, and distributed operations; NIST supports security/risk governance. Energy/carbon and AI workload economics are not directly supported.
sentence_reviews.notes: Add energy/carbon/cost optimization sources for exact coverage.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization is to connect Distributed systems & networking, Adversarial robustness & security testing to downstream capabilities including Large language models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support distributed systems and AI security/risk broadly; downstream LLM linkage is graph-derived.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible atlas positioning, not direct evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Operational reliability and risk management are relevant to large AI systems, but the exact LLM substrate claim is not sourced.
DDIA directly supports fault tolerance and distributed systems concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Strong conceptual support, but not exact phrase-level verification.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Distributed systems & networking, Adversarial robustness & security testing.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support operational and security concerns generally; numerical behavior and dependency list are not directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Template wording is broader than available citations.

sentence_reviews.text: Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization turns Distributed systems & networking into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports fault tolerance, replication, capacity, and distributed operations.

sentence_reviews.text: Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization turns Adversarial robustness & security testing into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports AI risk/security management broadly; adversarial testing linkage is indirect.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core security relationship is plausible, though exact adversarial-eval dependence needs a direct source.

sentence_reviews.text: Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization turns Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports capacity and operational control broadly but not GPU scheduling.

sentence_reviews.text: Fault tolerance, security, energy & cost optimization turns Optimization theory into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports optimization as a general AI concept; the operations linkage is not direct.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common dependency is plausible for cost/energy optimization.

edge_reviews.notes: Fault tolerance and capacity management are core distributed systems concerns.
Security testing is central to the security part of the node, though adversarial-eval evidence is broad.
Scheduling relates to cost/capacity operations, but GPU/admission-control support is not directly cited.
Optimization theory is a plausible common dependency for cost and energy optimization.
